issue.title: Reconciler: derive SHIP/completed from archived plan + closed GitHub issue; reconcile release entries

successCriteria.description: Archived plan + GitHub-closed + not in history: standalone entry gets an idempotent history record and is removed; a parentRelease child transitions to completed/SHIP and gets the history record

Either ship signal alone is insufficient (archived-but-open and closed-but-not-archived both leave the entry untouched); a failing/absent gh fetcher degrades silently with the rest of the pass unaffected

Release entries reconcile: version in history.recentReleases is removed; def archived but history missing gets a reconstructed release history record (issues from the archived def, tag = version) and is removed; in-flight and frozen release entries are untouched
Zero gh-fetcher invocations on passes with no ship-shaped entries (asserted via fake-fetcher call counting)
--print shows recorded-vs-derived rows for release entries and would-heal ship rows for issues, stays strictly read-only, and never fabricates drift when the fetcher is unavailable
All existing reconciler tests (29) plus new ones green; full framework suite green; live-fire --print and a real pass on this repo are clean

phases.title: Issue SHIP derivation (archived plan + gh-closed) with injectable fetcher
verification: All reconcile-state tests green (29 existing + new); only the dogfood-parity guard red (expected until phase 2)
Fake-fetcher call-count assertions prove the gh budget rule
History record shape matches state.mjs append-history output (number, title, completedAt, optional parentRelease)
phases.summary: Added fetchIssueStateViaGh(number) (exported; spawnSync gh, shell on win32, 5s timeout, OPEN/CLOSED/null, never throws) and threaded { fetchIssueState } through reconcile(). Ship-derivation in reconcileEntry sits after the history check, before in-flight advance: gated on new hasArchivedPlan() helper (fetcher called ONLY past that gate — budget rule), CLOSED → idempotent appendShipHistoryRecord + standalone delete ("ship-derived: removed") or legality-pre-guarded completed/SHIP for parentRelease children ("ship-derived: completed"); OPEN/null falls through. Safety contract amendment 4a. 9 new tests (heal, idempotency/history-first, release child single-change, archived+OPEN, budget never-called, fetcher-null isolation, frozen, zero-call in-flight fixture, dry-run); reconcile suite 38/38; framework 95/96 with only the expected dogfood-parity red (phase 2 regen). testResults: { framework: "node", command: "pnpm -C packages/framework test", passed: 95, failed: 1, skipped: 0, status: "failed" } — the 1 failure is the planned phase-boundary dogfood drift. Phase 2 hooks: hasArchivedPlan + appendShipHistoryRecord patterns reusable; buildReport gains { fetchIssueState } third param; release hook point is the non-issue continue line.

phases.title: Release reconciliation, --print rows, dogfood regen, live-fire
verification: Full framework suite green including dogfood-parity guard
Release teardown tests cover both v-prefix variants and idempotency
--print read-only assertion extended to release + ship-derivation rows; fetcher-null never fabricates drift
Live-fire: --print clean (no gh calls, no spurious rows), real pass changes []
phases.summary: reconcileReleaseEntry wired at the former non-issue continue: (a) version in history.recentReleases → entry removed; (b) archived def (both v-prefix variants, defensive parse, issues-from-def-wins) → idempotent release history record {version, issues, completedAt, tag} reconstructed + entry removed; (c) in-flight untouched; frozen respected. Safety contract amendment 5a. buildReport gained { fetchIssueState } third param (default null — programmatic callers never spawn gh; only --print passes fetchIssueStateViaGh): release rows (in-sync/in-history/archived-def) + ship-derived issue rows past the hasArchivedPlan gate; fetcher absent/null/OPEN → in-sync row with "archived plan; gh unavailable/open" note, drift never fabricated. 11 new tests (reconcile suite 49); dogfood regenerated; LIVE-FIRE: --print instant with zero gh calls, issue:271 in-sync executing/EXECUTE 2/2, no spurious rows; real pass changes []. Framework suite 107/107 incl. dogfood-parity. testResults: { framework: "node", command: "pnpm -C packages/framework test", passed: 107, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.
